message:
fix: resolve kubeconfig import cluster/user name collisions (#3651) (#3657)

When importing a kubeconfig, if a cluster or user name already exists
but with different data (e.g. different server URL or credentials), the
existing entry was silently kept. This caused the imported context to
use wrong credentials without any warning.

Now, when a name collision is detected with differing data, the imported
entry is automatically renamed (e.g. "my-cluster" -> "my-cluster-imported")
and the context reference is updated accordingly. If the data is identical,
the existing entry is reused without renaming.

Closes #3651

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
